diagram_name: Dimensionality and Density of States Explorer
chapter_title: "Bloch's Theorem, Band Formation, and E-k Diagrams"
learning_objective: Students will predict and compare g(E) in 3D, 2D, 1D, and 0D and explain (Understand, L2) how dimensionality reduction improves semiconductor laser performance.
specification:
Type: MicroSim **sim-id:** density-of-states-explorer
**Library:** p5.js
**Status:** Specified **Learning objective:** Students will predict and compare g(E) in 3D, 2D, 1D, and 0D and explain (Understand, L2) how dimensionality reduction improves semiconductor laser performance. **Canvas size:** 900 × 540 px, responsive. **Controls:** - Select dropdown: Dimensionality — "3D (Bulk)", "2D (Quantum Well)", "1D (Quantum Wire)", "0D (Quantum Dot)". Default: "3D". - Slider: Effective mass \(m^*/m_0\), 0.05–1.0, default 0.067. Label: "m*/m₀:" - Slider: Confinement dimension L, 1–20 nm, default 10 nm (active when not 3D). Label: "L (nm):" - Checkbox: "Show gain bandwidth" — overlays schematic optical gain curve width relative to DOS shape. **Visual layout:** Left panel (65%): Plot of g(E) vs. E from E_c to E_c + 0.4 eV. - 3D: smooth √(E-E_c) curve. - 2D: staircase with equal-height steps at E_n = n²·ħ²π²/(2m*L²). - 1D: spiky curve with 1/√(E-E_n) peaks at subband edges. - 0D: vertical arrows at discrete energies E_n. All normalized to same peak height. Color: 3D=blue, 2D=green, 1D=orange, 0D=red. Right panel (35%): Card showing dimensionality, g(E) formula, device application, laser threshold comparison (bulk > QW > QD). **Behavior:** All controls update the plot in real time. On resize: updateCanvasSize() reflows. **Implementation:** p5.js, single HTML file. Parent canvas to document.querySelector('main').
